vitals.terrain: Hilly with steep climbs and mountain passes

course_description.overview: Self-supported ultra cycling race from Mount Vesuvius near Naples, through Calabria, ferry across Strait of Messina, to Mount Etna in Sicily, featuring demanding climbs, coastal descents, and southern Italian landscapes.

key_segments.name: Vesuvius and Faito climbs
key_segments.description: Initial high-pace climbs from Ercolano, stretching the field early.

key_segments.name: Calabrian mountains
key_segments.description: Rugged terrain with tough passes, irregular roads, and elevation challenges.

key_segments.name: Strait of Messina ferry
key_segments.description: Transition to Sicily with quick refuel before night climbs.

key_segments.name: Etna south ascent
key_segments.description: Brutal final climb on rough lava terrain leading to finish in Catania.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Extreme endurance test with massive elevation, steep gradients, unpredictable weather, and full self-sufficiency over remote southern Italian roads.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Naples for start in Ercolano; finish in Catania with airport access.
logistics.registration_details: Open since September 2025 at www.twovolcanosprint.com; fees support environmental projects.
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly with bike to Naples; ship home from Catania if needed; fixed GPX route.
logistics.local_considerations: Ferry across Strait of Messina required; watch for traffic, shepherds, dogs; Italian road conditions variable with cobblestones and rain.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic self-supported odyssey linking Italy's iconic volcanoes for hardened ultra cyclists.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-distance riders seeking raw adventure, massive climbs, and southern Italy's wild beauty.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those craving a prestigious, unsupported challenge with stunning views outweighing the suffering.
pro_tips: Master self-sufficiency: pack repair kit, navigation, and food for remote stretches.
Train for repeated steep climbs and night riding.
Embrace Calabria's rugged reality over romanticized expectations.
